message:
fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths (ft-5m5xc)

## Defect: dead config field

crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62
ScanPipelineConfig declared `pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool` with
Default = true, but nothing in the crate ever read the flag:

  - process() at :405 unconditionally calls scan_newlines_and_ansi.
  - process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally calls
    scan_newlines_and_ansi_with_state.

An operator who set `enable_ansi_analysis = false` in their config
got zero behavior change — the documented off-switch lied about
what it controlled. Classic reality-gap.

## Fix

Zero `ansi_byte_count` in the scan output AFTER the SIMD pass when
the flag is false. The SIMD scan always computes both
newline_count and ansi_byte_count in a single pass (it's one
unrolled loop over the bytes), so splitting the scan into
newline-only vs both-on would be a much larger refactor with
performance implications. The minimal correctness fix is: keep
the scan path unchanged, but zero the ANSI field before it flows
into ScanMetricsSummary / downstream density math.

Consequence for callers:

  - Default (enable_ansi_analysis = true): no change.
  - enable_ansi_analysis = false: ansi_byte_count and ansi_density
    now surface as 0 in the returned ScanMetricsSummary, matching
    the documented intent.

Chunked path keeps the scan_state.in_escape / utf8-continuation
carry honest (scan still runs, state still advances) so that if
the config flag is ever flipped back to true mid-stream the
boundary state is correct. Only the accumulator sees zeros.

## Regression tests

`pipeline_honors_enable_ansi_analysis_false_ft_5m5xc`:
  - Build pipeline with enable_ansi_analysis=false.
  - Feed "line1\n<ANSI>\nline3\n".
  - Assert newline_count == 3, logical_lines == 3 (unaffected).
  - Assert ansi_byte_count == 0 AND ansi_density == 0.0.
  - Sanity pass with the default config: SAME input yields a
    non-zero ansi_byte_count. This proves the branch is actually
    doing work — pre-fix, both configs returned identical ANSI
    counts because the flag was ignored.

`chunked_pipeline_honors_enable_ansi_analysis_false_ft_5m5xc`:
  - Same contract for the chunked accumulator path:
    state.ansi_byte_count() must stay 0 across two process_chunk
    calls when the flag is false. Covers the second call site
    that was also ignoring the flag.

## Verification

frankenterm-core --lib remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing
compile errors (ft-kqidb / ft-uigpj / ft-s2u51 / ft-zv3u9).
Edit is purely additive: two `if !self.config.enable_ansi_analysis
{ metrics.ansi_byte_count = 0; }` guards plus two regression
tests. No call site changed, no new public surface, no behavior
change for the default config. Runs clean once any one of those
four blockers lifts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
